records.ety: [LL. collector one who collects: cf. F. collecteur.]

defs: One who collects things which are separate; esp., one who makes a business or practice of collecting works of art, objects in natural history, etc.; as, a collector of coins.
quotes: I digress into Soho to explore a bookstall. Methinks I have been thirty years a collector.

defs: A compiler of books; one who collects scattered passages and puts them together in one book.
quotes: Volumes without the collector's own reflections.

defs: An officer appointed and commissioned to collect and receive customs, duties, taxes, or toll.
quotes: A great part of this is now embezzled . . . by collectors, and other officers.

defs: A bachelor of arts in Oxford, formerly appointed to superintend some scholastic proceedings in Lent.
